data.content:
Now I have all the info. Let me collect the packages and resolve conflicts using the highest version:

- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 vs 8.0.4 → **10.0.1**
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 vs 1.11.4 → **1.13.2**
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting`: **8.0.1**
- `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting`: **1.15.0**
- `xunit`: **2.9.3**

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information. Let me collect all packages and their versions:

- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1 (Api, Shared, Tests), 8.0.4 (Worker) → highest: 10.0.1
- Azure.Identity: 1.13.2 (Api), 1.11.4 (Shared) → highest: 1.13.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 8.0.1 (Worker) → 8.0.1
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Worker) → 1.15.0


- xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests) → 2.9.3

Now I need to create a Directory.Packages.props file with centralized version management enabled and all the package versions defined, then strip out the Version attributes from each project file. Let me first check if this file already exists.

    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
